data.title: SerpApi sues SearchApi in U.S. District Court, alleging theft of proprietary tech. Feb 16, 2026.
data.description: In Austin, Texas on February 16, 2026, SerpApi initiated legal action in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas against Zilvinas Kucinskas and his company, SearchApi. The lawsuit alleges that Kucinskas and SearchApi unlawfully appropriated SerpApi's advanced technology and confidential information to develop a competing product. This legal dispute centers on the accusation of intellectual property theft and infringement.

This certification signifies a strong focus on protecting data and ensuring privacy in the digital realm.

data.title: MANSCAPED partners with Stavros Halkias to create a celebrity hairball monster for charity after Super Bowl debut.
data.description: In San Diego, MANSCAPED®, a leading men's grooming company, recently debuted their Super Bowl ad "Hair Ballad," featuring singing hairballs in a power ballad-style performance. The ad aired just before kickoff on NBC and captured the attention of a global audience. Following its success, the company has announced an extension of the campaign in collaboration with comedian Stavros Halkias. This partnership aims to further promote MANSCAPED®'s products and brand lifestyle. The original ad showcased the challenge of dealing with unwanted body hair in a creative and humorous way, resonating with viewers. MANSCAPED® continues to innovate in the men's grooming industry, leveraging unique marketing strategies to reach a wide audience. The success of "Hair Ballad" demonstrates the effectiveness of their approach, with the ad receiving significant attention and positive feedback. This collaboration with Stavros Halkias is expected to further drive brand awareness and engagement, solidifying MANSCAPED®'s position as a top player in the men's grooming market.

data.title: Fluor Corp's 2025 revenue was $4.175B with a $25.5B backlog. Plans $1.4B stock buyback in '26. Segment profit down 42%.
data.description: Fluor Corporation reported a net loss of $1.6 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025, compared to $1.9 billion in earnings in the previous year. The company's revenue was $4.175 billion, missing estimates of $4.249 billion, with a decrease from $4.260 billion in the prior year. Adjusted EPS was 33 cents, lower than the 34 cents estimated by analysts. New awards in the quarter were $1.127 billion, down from $2.308 billion in the prior year. Urban Solutions segment saw revenue increase to $2.630 billion, while Energy Solutions reported revenue of $943 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $91 million, and consolidated segment profit was $120 million. Shares of Fluor Corporation traded higher following the release of these financial results.

data.title: Reju and Goodwill of the Finger Lakes highlight Rochester's role in textile circularity. CEO, Jennifer Lake, and leaders showcase community efforts. (Word count: 23)
data.description: Patrik Frisk, CEO of Reju, joined by Jennifer Lake, President & CEO of Goodwill of the Finger Lakes, along with regional leaders, convened to emphasize community-focused circular initiatives in Rochester, New York on February 17, 2026. Reju, a company specializing in textile-to-textile rejuvenation, is spearheading efforts in the region.

data.title: Anthropic introduces Sonnet 4.6, with fears of traditional software being replaced by AI. Software stocks take a hit.
data.description: Anthropic has emerged as a leader in developing autonomous agentic systems, and the recent introduction of Claude Sonnet 4.6 on Tuesday has intensified the competition among software companies. Following closely after the Claude Opus 4.6 launch, Sonnet 4.6 has dealt a significant blow to traditional software firms, causing a stir in the market. This back-to-back release has had a notable impact on MSFT stock, prompting fluctuations in its value. Concerns are arising among software investors regarding the potential cannibalization effect, where the advanced AI agents such as those from Anthropic are seen as posing a threat to conventional user interfaces and subscription-based models. Anthropic addressed these concerns in a blog post accompanying the latest release, highlighting the challenge of automating older specialized software lacking modern interfaces like APIs. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Drivers underestimate car ownership costs by $4,500 annually due to gas and insurance expenses, per Synchrony survey.
data.description: According to a recent survey conducted by Synchrony and released on 17-Feb-2026, drivers are underestimating the annual cost of owning a car by over $4,500. The survey found that expenses related to gas and insurance are the primary contributors to the yearly expenditure on cars. This highlights the significant discrepancy between what drivers expect to pay and the actual costs associated with vehicle ownership. It is essential for car owners to have a more accurate understanding of these expenses to better manage their budgets and finances.

data.title: Prime Power Inc. launches reliable power generation platform for data centers, offering scalable solutions with utility-independent reliability.
data.description: In Houston, Prime Power Inc. has officially launched its power generation platform, aiming to provide dependable, scalable, and efficient energy solutions for the digital infrastructure sector. The company specializes in creating and running behind-the-meter power generation systems, offering reliability independent of utilities, quick deployment, and stable costs for data centers and critical facilities. With a growing need for secure and efficient energy solutions, Prime Power Inc. is set to meet market demands by leveraging its expertise in delivering reliable and cost-effective power solutions.

data.title: Genesis renews as title partner of the Genesis Invitational with a multi-year agreement ahead of event's 100th playing.
data.description: Renowned golfer Tiger Woods has revealed a multi-year partnership agreement leading up to the 100th edition of a historic tournament. The deal was disclosed in Pacific Palisades, Calif., and Ponte Vedra Beach, Fla., on Feb. 17, 2026. Genesis, The PGA TOUR, and TGR Live shared news of Genesis extending its commitment for several years.
